summary_text: **Findings (ordered by severity)** 1. **Critical: manual publish path bypasses scheduler locking and CAS.** `manual_publish_post()` posts directly after loading queue, with no `_post_due_jobs_lock()` and no `acquire_posting_lock()` step, so it can race the daemon on the same row. Refs: [manual_publish.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/manual_publish.py#L140), [manual_publish.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/manual_publish.py#L147), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L419), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L561) 2. **Critical: PID-file lock is brittle; with launchd `KeepAlive` this causes restart storms and slot misses.** `_acquire_pid_lock()` is plain PID-file + `kill(pid,0)` check, no advisory lock or atomic claim, so stale/reused PID and startup races are possible. On conflict it exits immediately, and launchd retries every 30s (`KeepAlive=true`, `ThrottleInterval=30`), which we observed in logs for hours. Refs: [scheduler_health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py#L167), [scheduler_health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler_health.py#L177), [com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist](/Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist#L7), [com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist](/Users/masayuki/Library/LaunchAgents/com.cursorvers.x-auto-scheduler.plist#L24), [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log#L20440) 3. **High: overdue handling is “drop to missed”, not recovery.** Posts older than `MAX_LATENESS_MINUTES` are excluded from due selection and then marked `missed` after 3 cycles; there is no automatic catch-up mode after scheduler downtime/collision. This directly explains the 4/6 11:50 miss behavior. Refs: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L64), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L193), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L518), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L528), [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log#L20974) 4. **Medium: status propagation latency/skip can re-surface terminal rows from Notion SoT.** Local `missed/failed/duplicate_blocked` changes are pushed to Notion only in periodic sync (5 min), and stale-snapshot checks can skip updates. During this gap, Notion reload can reintroduce previously terminal rows as `approved`, causing repeated alerts/oscillation. Refs: [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L476), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L608), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L632), [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log#L20979), [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log#L21070), [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log#L21077) 5. **Medium: `posted.json` archive write is atomic but not lock-protected.** `archive_posted()` uses `os.replace` but no file lock. Concurrent writer paths (daemon + manual publish) can still lose appended history, weakening duplicate guard and daily-limit accounting. Refs: [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L723), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L745), [duplicate_guard.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/duplicate_guard.py#L56) 6. **Medium: collision observability is partially blind.** Health collision check scans `scheduler.err.log` tail only; collision bursts are also in `scheduler.log` and can be missed depending on redirection/rotation behavior. Refs: [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L281), [x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L286), [scheduler.log](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/logs/scheduler.log#L20440) --- **Recommended structural fixes** 1. **Unify all publish entrypoints under one critical section.** Refactor manual publish to call the same core posting function used by scheduler, and e...
